Riots Communities and Victims Panel
暴動及受害者委員會

Executive summary
結論

Children and parents
家長及子女

We heard from many communities who feel that rioter behaviour could ultimately be ascribed to poor parenting (家長管教不足) . We need to consider what can be done to ensure that all children get the right support, control and guidance from parents or guardians to give them the best possible chance of making the most of their lives.

Widening inclusion – Some children grow up without a single positive adult figure in their lives. Public services should take steps (政府加強教育下一代) to ensure all children have a positive role model (from a child’s wider family or from the local community). Where it is in the best interests of the child to do so, we recommend that absent fathersshould be contacted by statutory social services and schools about their children as amatter of course.

Youth unemployment
青年失業

Our Neighbourhood Survey found that 83 per cent of people feel that youth unemployment
is a problem within their local area, and 71 per cent of residents feel that there are insufficient
employment opportunities for young people.

The riots were particularly characterised by opportunistic looting and very much targeted
at brands – 50 per cent of recorded offences in the riots were acquisitive in nature.4 The Panel
was told that the majority of shops targeted stocked high value consumer products: clothes, trainers, mobile telephones and computers. (搶錢及名牌)

Police and the public
市民不信任警察

Increasing trust in the police Trust in the police is vitally important in any
community. It leads to communities getting more involved in policing, it ensures the police
can understand local communities’ needs and it helps to break down cultural barriers. When
the public trust police motives, they are willing to support them by reporting crimes or antisocial behaviour, by providing local intelligence and acting as witnesses.

The Panel recommends that police forces should deploy more manpower (投放更多人手) to proactively engage with communities about issues that impact on the perceptions of their integrity.


Wealth inequality and responsible capitalism
仇富情緒

Over half of respondents to the Panel’s Neighbourhood Survey believe there is a growing gap between rich and poor in their
local area. The Panel believe society must continue to support sustainable growth and promote business expansion. However,
alongside this, we believe that businesses have a clear role giving something back to society and making progressive steps to sharing
wealth and providing opportunities for individuals to achieve a stake in business.

林鄭想借報告將運動spin走做黑人社區暴動


2011年英國騷亂，有傳媒稱為倫敦之炎[12]、倫敦暴動[13]，是2011年8月6日晚上在英國首都倫敦開始的一系列社會騷亂事件[14]，一直持續到8月10日才平息。騷亂的導火索是2011年8月4日在倫敦北部的托登罕（Tottenham），一名29歲的黑人男性平民馬克·達根（Mark Duggan）被倫敦警察廳的警務人員槍殺[15][16]。之後民眾上街抗議警察暴行[17]。由於受害人的非裔背景，移民問題自然首當其衝，也有當地人認為英國的經濟不景氣，加上政府撙節支出，衝擊托登罕等高失業率地區，才是引發社會不穩定的主要原因[14]。

史與現實因素
此次騷亂是自1995年發生於布里克斯頓的騷亂至今，英國最嚴重的一次動亂事件[15]，更是自倫敦大轟炸（1940年9月7日至1941年5月10日，二戰其間納粹德國轟炸倫敦）後，倫敦發生的波及人數最多且最緊張的事件[21]。

騷亂的起源與當地黑人社區同警察之間的緊張關係有很大關係，這與英國其他具有很大黑人移民人口的城市類似，例如伯明罕就曾因Kingsley Burrell的死引發過示威活動[2][22][23]。記者把這起騷亂與發生於1985年10月6日的騷亂：當時一名黑人婦女在警方突擊她的住家時，由於受到驚嚇心臟病爆發而身亡，結果引起當地黑人居民的不滿，最終釀成大約500名黑人攻擊警察、搶劫和縱火，導致一名叫Keith Blakelock警察殉職的大規模暴動事件——相聯繫，因為它也是發生在托登罕[24][25]。

現實因素包括當地是個多族裔混居區——它聚集了大量的非洲－加勒比、西非、庫爾德、土耳其裔賽普勒斯、土耳其、愛爾蘭和葡萄牙後裔的移民；近三十年來，區內各族關係緊張，而非洲－加勒比裔同當地警察之間更是長期處於緊張狀態。該地具有比較高的貧窮與失業率，作為倫敦的一個貧困區，當地的失業率比全國平均水平高出近兩倍，人均壽命比倫敦平均水平低5歲，各類犯罪較為多發。富人與窮人之間的隔閡，很低的社會流動，以及民眾對政府為了減少債務而削減公共開支的不滿[26][27][28]。

射殺馬克·達根

Ferry Lane，2010年3月22日
槍殺事件發生於8月4日晚上，當時警察正準備對乘坐在一輛計程車車裡的馬克·達根實施逮捕，地點位於托登罕地鐵站附近的一座橋上——Ferry Lane bridge[29][30]。

該事件目前由獨立警察投訴委員會調查處理[29]。該委員會負責處理民眾死於警察的事件。目前還不完全確定警察當時為何要逮捕馬克·達根，IPCC表示逮捕行動是打擊槍枝犯罪行動的一部分，而馬克·達根是警方懷疑的一個與古柯鹼交易有關的持槍的罪犯[31]。

馬克·達根的親屬和朋友宣稱馬克當時並沒有攜帶武器[32]。但IPCC則表示馬克·達根當時攜帶著一把手槍[33][34]。

事件發生過後，媒體普遍報導發現有一顆子彈嵌入在警察的無線對講機里，這暗示了馬克曾對警察開槍[35]。《衛報》報導馬克使用的是中空彈[35][36]。

示威活動
8月6日舉行了一場和平示威活動，起點始於Broadwater農場終止於托登罕警察局[37]。這起示威是由死者的親人和朋友發起的，其中包括當地居民和社區負責人，目的是要求獲得真相和公正[31][38][39]，大約有200人參加[15]。後來，一名警官同示威群眾談話，不過他們要求同更高職位的長官來跟他們談。由於對警方的答覆不滿意，所以他們站在警局前面的幾個小時比他們原計劃的時間要長。根據目擊者稱，在傍晚有一個更年輕且更激動的人群也來到了那裡，其中一些人手裡拿著武器。當他們聽說警察攻擊了一名16歲的女孩時暴力活動就開始了[2][15]。大約20:20（當地時間），其中的一些人開始攻擊附近的兩輛警車並將之點燃。之後騷亂在該地開始蔓延，隨後波及到倫敦其他地區，乃至倫敦以外的其他英格蘭城市[40][41]。

8月6日舉行了一場和平示威活動，起點始於Broadwater農場終止於托登罕警察局[37]。這起示威是由死者的親人和朋友發起的，其中包括當地居民和社區負責人，目的是要求獲得真相和公正[31][38][39]，大約有200人參加[15]。後來，一名警官同示威群眾談話，不過他們要求同更高職位的長官來跟他們談。由於對警方的答覆不滿意，所以他們站在警局前面的幾個小時比他們原計劃的時間要長。根據目擊者稱，在傍晚有一個更年輕且更激動的人群也來到了那裡，其中一些人手裡拿著武器。當他們聽說警察攻擊了一名16歲的女孩時暴力活動就開始了[2][15]。大約20:20（當地時間），其中的一些人開始攻擊附近的兩輛警車並將之點燃。之後騷亂在該地開始蔓延，隨後波及到倫敦其他地區，乃至倫敦以外的其他英格蘭城市[40][41]。

搶劫
位於托登罕的商戶所受的經濟損失達數百萬英鎊[63]。此外，騷亂也對一些歷史建築造成了難以彌補的破壞[64]。

許多報導說在不少地方發現了大量暴亂者掠奪到的贓物，這被懷疑為有計劃有組織實施的。估計各地被劫掠到的財產總計達1億英鎊[65]。倫敦警察廳已派出450個警探負責逮捕這些罪犯[66]。搶劫者的照片已被公布在警察廳的網站上[67]。

英國保險業協會（The Association of British Insurers）估計，騷亂對工商業造成的損失將達到數千萬英鎊。保險公司們估計將要賠付超過2億英鎊的賠償金[68]。

英國零售協會（BRC）9日估計，這次暴亂對英國零售業所造成的經濟損失，將多達數千萬英鎊。

Riots Communities and Victims Panel
暴動及受害者委員會

Executive summary
結論

Children and parents
家長及子女

We heard from many communities who feel that rioter behaviour could ultimately be ascribed to poor parenting (家長管教不足) . We need to consider what can be done to ensure that all children get the right support, control and guidance from parents or guardians to give them the best possible chance of making the most of their lives.

Widening inclusion – Some children grow up without a single positive adult figure in their lives. Public services should take steps (政府加強教育下一代) to ensure all children have a positive role model (from a child’s wider family or from the local community). Where it is in the best interests of the child to do so, we recommend that absent fathersshould be contacted by statutory social services and schools about their children as amatter of course.

Youth unemployment
青年失業

Our Neighbourhood Survey found that 83 per cent of people feel that youth unemployment
is a problem within their local area, and 71 per cent of residents feel that there are insufficient
employment opportunities for young people.

The riots were particularly characterised by opportunistic looting and very much targeted
at brands – 50 per cent of recorded offences in the riots were acquisitive in nature.4 The Panel
was told that the majority of shops targeted stocked high value consumer products: clothes, trainers, mobile telephones and computers. (搶錢及名牌)

Police and the public
市民不信任警察

Increasing trust in the police Trust in the police is vitally important in any
community. It leads to communities getting more involved in policing, it ensures the police
can understand local communities’ needs and it helps to break down cultural barriers. When
the public trust police motives, they are willing to support them by reporting crimes or antisocial behaviour, by providing local intelligence and acting as witnesses.

The Panel recommends that police forces should deploy more manpower (投放更多人手) to proactively engage with communities about issues that impact on the perceptions of their integrity.


Wealth inequality and responsible capitalism
仇富情緒

Over half of respondents to the Panel’s Neighbourhood Survey believe there is a growing gap between rich and poor in their
local area. The Panel believe society must continue to support sustainable growth and promote business expansion. However,
alongside this, we believe that businesses have a clear role giving something back to society and making progressive steps to sharing
wealth and providing opportunities for individuals to achieve a stake in business.

Riots Communities and Victims Panel
暴動及受害者委員會

Executive summary
結論

Children and parents
家長及子女

We heard from many communities who feel that rioter behaviour could ultimately be ascribed to poor parenting (家長管教不足) . We need to consider what can be done to ensure that all children get the right support, control and guidance from parents or guardians to give them the best possible chance of making the most of their lives.

Widening inclusion – Some children grow up without a single positive adult figure in their lives. Public services should take steps (政府加強教育下一代) to ensure all children have a positive role model (from a child’s wider family or from the local community). Where it is in the best interests of the child to do so, we recommend that absent fathersshould be contacted by statutory social services and schools about their children as amatter of course.

Youth unemployment
青年失業

Our Neighbourhood Survey found that 83 per cent of people feel that youth unemployment
is a problem within their local area, and 71 per cent of residents feel that there are insufficient
employment opportunities for young people.

The riots were particularly characterised by opportunistic looting and very much targeted
at brands – 50 per cent of recorded offences in the riots were acquisitive in nature.4 The Panel
was told that the majority of shops targeted stocked high value consumer products: clothes, trainers, mobile telephones and computers. (搶錢及名牌)

Police and the public
市民不信任警察

Increasing trust in the police Trust in the police is vitally important in any
community. It leads to communities getting more involved in policing, it ensures the police
can understand local communities’ needs and it helps to break down cultural barriers. When
the public trust police motives, they are willing to support them by reporting crimes or antisocial behaviour, by providing local intelligence and acting as witnesses.

The Panel recommends that police forces should deploy more manpower (投放更多人手) to proactively engage with communities about issues that impact on the perceptions of their integrity.


Wealth inequality and responsible capitalism
仇富情緒

Over half of respondents to the Panel’s Neighbourhood Survey believe there is a growing gap between rich and poor in their
local area. The Panel believe society must continue to support sustainable growth and promote business expansion. However,
alongside this, we believe that businesses have a clear role giving something back to society and making progressive steps to sharing
wealth and providing opportunities for individuals to achieve a stake in business.
